reference_answer: Service Zone 2.
Service Zone 3.
Power supply fault.
Routine sampler maintenance.
Operations Officer K. Alvarez.
D. Pham.
M. Cortes, Communications Officer.
2026-04-29 03:45 (one hour earlier than the as-logged 04:45 because the sampler controller clock had drifted one hour ahead; corrected under §6.5).
2026-04-29 04:45 as logged on the local controller; this timestamp is not operative for rule computations.
2026-05-01 17:00 (Friday). 24 hours of clear-sample time accumulated across two active segments separated by a §6.2a pause from 09:00 to 11:00 on 2026-05-01.
2026-05-01 17:30, when Operations Officer Alvarez logged §6.1 satisfied (event E-11).
2026-05-04 17:00. The unshifted Sunday deadline shifts to close of business (17:00) on the next business day, Monday 2026-05-04.
6 days, 4 hours (from issuance at 2026-04-28 08:00 to lift at 2026-05-04 12:00).
6 days, 3 hours, 30 minutes (from issuance at 2026-04-28 09:30 to lift at 2026-05-04 13:00).
10 hours, 35 minutes (from corrected E-04 time 2026-04-29 03:45 to E-05 at 2026-04-29 14:20).
2026-05-01 09:00 to 2026-05-01 11:00 (the E-08 / E-09 maintenance window; the §6.2a pause is the only pause that occurred during the active clear-sample countdown).
2 (the power-supply-fault interval starting at corrected 03:45 on 2026-04-29 and the maintenance interval on 2026-05-01).
13 (E-01 through E-13).
3 (engineering review report on contamination cause; procedure review on OPS-PROJ-04-29; and formal filing of the metrology technician's clock-drift report).
Offline (since 03:45 corrected). The first sampler-offline interval was in progress.
Lifted. The BWN was lifted at 2026-05-04 12:00, and 13:00 on the same date is after that lift time.
Paused under §6.2a, with 18 hours counted at that moment.
Operational. The sampler had been restored at 2026-04-29 14:20 and was operational through 2026-05-01 09:00.

No. OPS-PROJ-04-29 was an internal projection circulated 2026-04-29 16:00 and was conditional on no further positive samples; it was not a binding announcement, schedule, or commitment, and the packet explicitly states it should not be cited as one.
The second positive sample (event E-06) on 2026-04-30 09:00 superseded the projection by triggering a §6.2 clear-sample-clock reset.
§6.5 (Timestamp correction).
§6.2a (Clock pause on sampler offline).
§6.3 (specifically the weekend/holiday clause that shifts the deadline to close of business 17:00 on the next business day).

Not yet issued as of packet close on 2026-05-05; expected by 2026-05-19. Preliminary attribution is a main-line break upstream of Station S-3, but final attribution awaits the written engineering review report and may revise the cause.
Scheduled for 2026-05-13; the review's findings have not been issued as of packet close.
No. Under §6.2a a sampler-offline pause does not constitute a positive sample and does not trigger §6.2; only the actual positive sample at E-06 on 2026-04-30 09:00 triggered the reset.
No. The RUN closure on 2026-05-04 13:00 is recorded under E-13 and is not a separate Lift Notice; LFT-2026-05-04 is the only Lift Notice issued and applies to the BWN in Zone 2.
19 hours (the unshifted deadline would have been 2026-05-03 17:00 and the lift was issued 2026-05-04 12:00; this is the figure stated in §4).
